title: Unit 14 Hyper-Exhaustive Masterclass: The Past Tenses (Imperfect vs. Aorist)

script.text: Welcome... to the Hyper-Exhaustive Grammar Masterclass for Unit 14: The Past Tenses. This is the capstone of our A2 Expansion stage. You’ve learned how to build stems and how to talk about the future. Now... we look back. I'm William... and here with me is Athina. Athina... why does the Greek past feel like a time machine?

Γεια σας! It’s all about the 'lens' you choose. In Greek... just like the future... you must decide if you are describing a 'Video' or a 'Photo'. The 'Video' is the Imperfect tense... things you used to do regularly. The 'Photo' is the Aorist... or Simple Past... for things that happened once and finished.
The choice is binary. Now... let's look at the most iconic feature of the Greek past... the 'Augment'. That little 'e' sound at the front of the word. Athina... what is the 'Rule of 3'?

This is a rhythmic rule. Past tense verbs MUST have the stress on the third syllable from the end. If the word is too short... like 'ghráp-sa'... it only has two syllables. So we glue an 'E' to the front to make it three! 'É-ghrap-sa'. But look... if the word is long enough on its own... like 'ghráp-sa-me'... we don't need the 'E' anymore! It disappears!
It’s a rhythmic crutch. And if the verb already starts with a vowel... like 'el-pée-zo'... to hope... it doesn't add an 'E'. Instead... the vowel stretches. Athina?
Yes. The 'E' at the start becomes a long 'EE' sound... 'eél-pi-za'. I was hoping. This is called a Vocalic Augment.

Fascinating. Now... let’s conjugate. Both past tenses actually share the EXACT same endings. The only difference is the stem you use. Let's start with the Aorist... the Simple Past... using the 'Photo' stem. Athina... conjugate 'é-paée-kso'.
Έπαιξα... έπαιξες... έπαιξε. Έπαιξαμε... έπαιξατε... έπαιξα. Listen to the rhythm... 'É-pa-k-sa'... 'É-pa-k-ses'. The 'E' is only there when we need it.
And the Imperfect? Using the PRESENT stem?
Exactly the same endings... but with the 'Video' stem. 'É-pai-za'... 'É-pai-zes'... 'É-pai-ze'. 'É-pai-za'... I used to play. Every single day.
So one change in the stem... and you move from 'I played once' to 'I used to play'. But we have some 'Shape-shifters' here too. The irregulars. What are the 'Big Five' past tenses?

You must memorize these: 'Vlé-po' becomes 'eée-tha'... I saw. 'Lé-o' becomes 'eée-pa'... I said. 'Pée-no' becomes 'eée-pya'... I drank. 'Pee-ghé-no' becomes 'pée-gha'... I went. And 'Ér-kho-mai' becomes 'eér-tha'... I came.
They completely change their appearance! And finally... the 'Defective' verbs. 'EE-me' and 'É-kho'. Why can't they take a 'Photo'?
Because being and having are states... they are never momentary snapshots. So they only have one past form. 'Eée-kha'... I had. And 'Ee-moun'... I was. You cannot use them in the Aorist!

They are strictly 'Video' verbs. You have now completed the exhaustive Masterclass for Unit 14... and with it... the entire A2 Expansion Stage. You can pluralize... describe... use the future... and now... recount the past. You have reached a massive milestone. Review the routines and hobbies vocabulary to practice your new past tense skills. Excellent work... we’ll see you in the Intermediate stage... starting with Unit 15: The Genitive Case.
Συγχαρητήρια! Τα καταφέρατε. Ραντεβού στην επόμενη ενότητα.
